exercises.title: Unit Test Suite
exercises.description: Write a comprehensive unit test suite for a `BankAccount` class. Cover: normal operations (deposit, withdraw, balance), edge cases (zero amounts, exact balance), and error cases (overdraft, negative deposit). Practice the testing pyramid by writing many small, focused tests.
exercises.expectedOutput:
PASS: test_initial_balance
PASS: test_initial_balance_defaults_zero
PASS: test_deposit_increases_balance
PASS: test_deposit_records_transaction
PASS: test_withdraw_decreases_balance
PASS: test_withdraw_exact_balance
PASS: test_withdraw_overdraft_raises
PASS: test_deposit_zero_raises
PASS: test_negative_initial_balance_raises
PASS: test_multiple_transactions

Result: 10/10 tests passed
hints: The BankAccount class is fully implemented — just run the tests to verify they all pass.
Each test creates a fresh BankAccount instance — tests are isolated.
For exception tests, use try/except to check that the right exception type is raised.

exercises.title: Test-Driven Development
exercises.description: Practice TDD: write tests FIRST, then implement the code to make them pass. You are given 5 failing tests for a `TextProcessor` class. Implement the class to make all tests pass. The tests define the expected behavior.
exercises.starterCode:
# Tests are already written — implement TextProcessor to pass them all

class TextProcessor:
    """Implement this class to pass all tests below."""

    def word_count(self, text):
        # TODO: Count words (split on whitespace, ignore empty strings)
        pass

    def most_common_words(self, text, n=3):
        # TODO: Return list of (word, count) tuples for top n words
        # Lowercase, strip punctuation: [,.!?;:]
        # Ignore common stopwords: ['the', 'a', 'an', 'in', 'is', 'it', 'to', 'of']
        pass

    def sentence_count(self, text):
        # TODO: Count sentences (end with . ! or ?)
        pass

    def avg_word_length(self, text):
        # TODO: Average length of all words (lowercase, strip punctuation)
        # Return float rounded to 2 decimal places
        pass

    def is_palindrome(self, text):
        # TODO: Check if text (ignoring spaces, case, punctuation) is a palindrome
        pass

def run_test(func):
    try:
        func()
        print(f"PASS: {func.__name__}")
        return True
    except AssertionError as e:
        print(f"FAIL: {func.__name__}: {e}")
        return False

processor = TextProcessor()

def test_word_count():
    assert processor.word_count("Hello world") == 2
    assert processor.word_count("  one  two  three  ") == 3
    assert processor.word_count("") == 0

def test_most_common_words():
    text = "the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog the dog"
    common = processor.most_common_words(text, 3)
    # 'the' appears 3x, 'dog' appears 2x, others 1x
    # 'the' is a stopword so excluded
    assert common[0][0] == "dog"
    assert common[0][1] == 2
    assert len(common) == 3

def test_sentence_count():
    text = "Hello world. How are you? I am fine! Yes."
    assert processor.sentence_count(text) == 4

def test_avg_word_length():
    result = processor.avg_word_length("cat and dog")
    assert result == 3.0  # (3 + 3 + 3) / 3

def test_is_palindrome():
    assert processor.is_palindrome("racecar") == True
    assert processor.is_palindrome("A man a plan a canal Panama") == True
    assert processor.is_palindrome("Hello world") == False

tests = [test_word_count, test_most_common_words, test_sentence_count,
         test_avg_word_length, test_is_palindrome]

passed = sum(run_test(t) for t in tests)
print(f"\nResult: {passed}/{len(tests)} tests passed")
exercises.expectedOutput:
PASS: test_word_count
PASS: test_most_common_words
PASS: test_sentence_count
PASS: test_avg_word_length
PASS: test_is_palindrome

Result: 5/5 tests passed
hints: word_count: len([w for w in text.split() if w])
most_common_words: import re; words = re.sub(r'[,.!?;:]', '', text).lower().split(); filter stopwords; use Counter
avg_word_length: words = re.sub(r'[,.!?;:]', '', text).lower().split(); return round(sum(len(w) for w in words) / len(words), 2)
is_palindrome: cleaned = re.sub(r'[^a-z]', '', text.lower()); return cleaned == cleaned[::-1]

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Exception Testing
